pattern: Closed-form analysis of a path graph with one shortcut: categorize node pairs by whether the shortcut is used, and express each category's distance-distribution analytically rather than running BFS per node.
hints: Without the shortcut x-y, all pairs have linear distance |a - b|; the shortcut creates two entry points to a cycle, letting some pairs detour through it.

Ensure x < y (swap if needed); the shortcut splits the chain into three segments: left [1..x], bridge [x..y] (length y-x), and right [y..n]. For a pair (a, b), compute both the direct chain distance and the shortcut-assisted distance, take the minimum.
For large n, group pairs by case (both left of x, one on each side, etc.) and accumulate counts analytically in O(n) time rather than n BFS runs.

edge_inputs.description: x equals y, no useful shortcut

stress.description: moderate n with various shortcut positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 500)
    x = rng.randint(1, n)
    y = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, x, y]
